failure_modes.text: Antifragility fails when people romanticize volatility and expose the system to shocks that are too large to survive.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when people romanticize volatility and expose the system to ruinous shocks that are too large to survive or learn from.
failure_modes.mitigation: Stay within the circle of competence and enforce intellectual humility about which shocks the system can absorb; bound exposure so failure is survivable.

failure_modes.text: Expert overconfidence can make the system brittle by substituting familiar models for the novel structure of the problem.
failure_modes.mitigation: Practice mental agility by asking 'what would you have to believe?' to accept an alternative viewpoint that contradicts the current hypothesis.

failure_modes.text: Analysis perfectionism can stall the experimentation and feedback loops that antifragility actually requires.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use rigorous inversion — start from what could destroy the system and size the response, rather than perfecting analysis of what might go right.

premortem_questions.text: What would need to be true for this idea to work?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would need to be true for this idea to work?

premortem_questions.text: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.text: What do we actually know, and where are we still guessing about the risk?

heuristics.text: Define the anti-goal and design against catastrophe before chasing upside.
Run a portfolio of experiments and compare outcomes instead of betting everything on one forecast.
Use direct engagement and feedback to keep improving the design through real-world stress and correction.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on anti-goal logic, bounded experimentation, and feedback-driven improvement rather than on volatility bravado.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor investing rhetoric because the raw source already offers stronger intervention chunks around anti-goals, experiments, and de-risking.
open_questions: The third premortem item is normalized from the de-risking section because the source is stronger on the practice than on a single direct question.

allies: The raw ally section is strong on systems-thinking and first-principles-thinking as later relation material.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is strong on confirmation-bias and availability-style substitution, but only the cleanest canonical ids should survive Wave 3.
structured_tensions: The source explicitly preserves a structured tension against anchoring.
